subject: Physics
title: Curved Spacetime Explained
description: A 60-second lesson on Curved Spacetime Explained.

hook.text: Imagine rolling a marble on a flat sheet. It goes straight. Now put a heavy ball on the sheet.

buildup.text: The marble curves toward the heavy ball—not because of a force, but because the surface is bent.

discovery.text: Mass does the same to spacetime. Planets curve the space around them, guiding nearby objects.

twist.text: Even light follows the curves. That's why starlight bends near the Sun—space itself is curved.

climax.text: Einstein wrote equations describing exactly how mass tells spacetime to curve.

punchline.text: Mass bends space. Bent space guides mass. A cosmic dance.

quiz.question: In general relativity, why do planets orbit the Sun?
options: They follow curves in spacetime created by the Sun's mass
The Sun's magnetic field pulls them
They are pushed by solar wind pressure
